zh-hans.lifeSummary: Ellen Hammone于大约1535年出生在英国肯特可藍別盧可。她于1562年11月30日与Richard Allarde在英国肯特可藍別盧可结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她埋葬在英国英格兰肯特比登登。

nameMeanings.name: Hammone
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English, Old French personal name Ha(i)mon, the oblique case form of the ancient Germanic Ha(i)mo, a short form of various compound names beginning with haim ‘home’. It frequently developed excrescent -d, giving Hamond, Haimund, and Hawmond. Alternatively, the name could derive from the Middle English personal name Hamund (Old Norse Hámundr, composed of the elements hár ‘high’ + mund ‘protection’), which may have been used in Normandy and in 12th-century eastern England, but the former explanation is more likely. The surname was sometimes confused with Almond and Ammon .

English: in the Bradford area of Yorkshire, the name is a shortened form of Ormondroyd, formerly Hamondesrode, from a lost place in Birstall (Yorkshire), named with the Middle English (Old French) personal name Hamon (1 above) + Middle English roid, a southern Yorkshire pronunciation of Old English rod ‘clearing’.
Irish: generally an importation from England, but occasionally an adopted name for Mac Ámoinn, see McCammon .

variants.name: Hammonds
meanings: English (Shropshire and Staffordshire): variant of Hammond , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Almond
meanings: English:
from the Middle English personal name Almund, in which Old English Æthelmund ‘noble protector’ and Ealhmund ‘temple protector’ have coalesced.
variant of   Allman   and   Allmand   both meaning ‘German’, assimilated by folk etymology to the vocabulary word denoting the tree.

variants.name: Hammett
meanings: English (southwestern England, especialy Devon): from the Middle English personal name Hamet, a pet form of ancient Germanic Hamo (see Hammond 1).

variants.name: Hamon
meanings: Breton, French, and English (Channel Islands): from the Breton, Old French, Middle English personal name Hamon, the oblique case form of the ancient Germanic Ha(i)mo, a short form of various compound names beginning with haim ‘home’. See also Hammond .

variants.name: Hamlin
meanings: English and Irish: of Norman origin, from the Norman French and Middle English personal name Hamelin, a double diminutive of Hamo (see Hammond ).
French: variant of   Hamelin   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
Americanized form of Finnish Hämäläinen (see Hamalainen ).

variants.name: Hampson
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire): patronymic from the Norman personal name Hamo, Hamon (see Hammond ).
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hAmhsaigh ‘descendant of Amhsach’ a byname meaning ‘mercenary soldier’ or ‘messenger’, from the adjective amhasach ‘aggressive’.

variants.name: Haimes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Yacov, Yosef.

English: variant, with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s, of Haime, of Norman origin, from the Old French personal name Haim (ancient Germanic Haimo, from haim- ‘home’), often Latinized as Hamo. Compare Hammond .
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

variants.name: Hammon
meanings: English (Middlesex and Ken): variant of   Hammond  .

nameMeanings.name: Ellen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Helen , although now no longer associated with that name. Initial H- tended to be added and dropped rather capriciously, leading to many doublets ( compare for example Esther and Hester ). It is also sometimes used as a short form of Eleanor , to which, however, it is unrelated.

variants.name: Eleanor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: Ellen Hammone was born about 1535, in Cranbrook, Kent, England. She married Richard Allarde on 30 November 1562, in Cranbrook, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. She was buried in Biddenden, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Ellen Hammone est née environ 1535, à Cranbrook, Kent, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Richard Allarde le 30 novembre 1562, à Cranbrook, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Elleenterrée à Biddenden, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Ellen Hammone wurde um 1535 in Cranbrook, Kent, England geboren. Sie hat am 30. November 1562 in Cranbrook, Kent, England Richard Allarde geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie wurde in Biddenden, Kent, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Ellen Hammone è nata il circa 1535 nel Cranbrook, Kent, England. Lei sposato Richard Allarde il 30 novembre 1562, nel Cranbrook, Kent, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie. Fu sepolta a Biddenden, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Ellen Hammoneは約1535年にイギリスケントクランブルークで生まれた。1562年11月30日にイギリスケントクランブルークでRichard Allardeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスイギリスケントビッドエンデンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Ellen Hammone(은)는 대략 1535년 영국 켄트 크랜브룩에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1562년 11월 30일 영국 켄트 크랜브룩에서 Richard Allarde(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 비데든에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Ellen Hammone nasceu aproximadamente 1535, em Cranbrook, Kent, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Richard Allarde em 30 de novembro de 1562, em Cranbrook, Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela foi sepultada em Biddenden, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Ellen Hammone родилась около 1535 г. в местности кранбрук, кэнт, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Allarde 30 ноября 1562 г. в местности кранбрук, кэнт, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Она была похоронена в местности бидденден, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Ellen Hammone nació aproximadamente 1535, en Cranbrook, Kent, Inglaterra. Se casó con Richard Allarde el 30 de noviembre de 1562, en Cranbrook, Kent, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas. Fue sepultada en Biddenden, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
